2604.18204.title: Hard to Be Heard: Phoneme-Level ASR Analysis of Phonologically Complex, Low-Resource Endangered Languages
2604.18204.paper_summary: We present a phoneme-level analysis of automatic speech recognition (ASR) for two low-resourced and phonologically complex East Caucasian languages, Archi and Rutul, based on curated and standardized speech-transcript resources totaling approximately 50 minutes and 1 hour 20 minutes of audio, respectively. Existing recordings and transcriptions are consolidated and processed into a form suitable for ASR training and evaluation. We evaluate several state-of-the-art audio and audio-language models, including wav2vec2, Whisper, and Qwen2-Audio. For wav2vec2, we introduce a language-specific phoneme vocabulary with heuristic output-layer initialization, which yields consistent improvements and achieves performance comparable to or exceeding Whisper in these extremely low-resource settings. Beyond standard word and character error rates, we conduct a detailed phoneme-level error analysis. We find that phoneme recognition accuracy strongly correlates with training frequency, exhibiting a characteristic sigmoid-shaped learning curve. For Archi, this relationship partially breaks for Whisper, pointing to model-specific generalization effects beyond what is predicted by training frequency. Overall, our results indicate that many errors attributed to phonological complexity are better explained by data scarcity. These findings demonstrate the value of phoneme-level evaluation for understanding ASR behavior in low-resource, typologically complex languages.
2604.18204.paper_summary_zh: 我們針對兩種資源稀缺且音系複雜的東高加索語言——阿爾奇語和魯圖爾語，進行了自動語音識別（ASR）的音素級分析，基於精心策劃和標準化的語音轉錄資源，總計約50分鐘和1小時20分鐘的音頻。現有的錄音和轉錄被整合並處理成適合ASR訓練和評估的形式。我們評估了幾種最先進的音頻和音頻-語言模型，包括wav2vec2、Whisper和Qwen2-Audio。對於wav2vec2，我們引入了一個語言特定的音素詞彙，並使用啟發式輸出層初始化，這帶來了一致的改進，並在這些極度資源稀缺的環境中達到與Whisper相當或超過的性能。除了標準的單詞和字符錯誤率外，我們還進行了詳細的音素級錯誤分析。我們發現音素識別準確率與訓練頻率之間存在強相關性，顯示出特徵性S形學習曲線。對於阿爾奇語，這種關係在Whisper中部分破裂，指向模型特定的泛化效應，超出了訓練頻率的預測。總體而言，我們的結果表明，許多歸因於音系複雜性的錯誤更好地可以用數據稀缺性來解釋。這些發現展示了音素級評估在理解資源稀缺、類型學上複雜語言中ASR行為的價值。
2604.18204.author: V. S. D. S. Mahesh Akavarapu et.al.
2604.18204.authors: V. S. D. S. Mahesh Akavarapu, Michael Daniel, Gerhard Jäger
